notes: Researched via WebSearch. Avoided Yelp/Tripadvisor/Instagram/Facebook. Preferred website subpages (menu/shop/catering) and review blogs (Tastet, BlogTO, eventsource) over bare homepages. Five vendors flagged null (small home bakers / no findable web presence + IG-only).

results.source_reasoning: The catering package PDF is image-rich (food trays, platters) — the bare homepage was the cookie-banner offender.
The .ca/en site (the actual corporate domain since 1951) is the product/butcher page — mehadrinmeats.ca is the storefront with the cookie banner that returned junk.
RestoMontreal listing has photos of challah/knishes/cakes — the bare homepage at montrealkosher.ca returned only a header.
Tastet review of the deli has multiple food photos (smoked meat, sandwiches, sides) — homepage was junk.
The /shop subpage has product photos (soups, salads, entrées) whereas the homepage was the cookie-banner offender.
The /fruit-arrangements catalog page is image-rich with product shots; the bare homepage returned UI chrome.

Indigo doesn't carry yahrzeit candles — best available is a single candle product page (Candle Warmer) which gives an isolated product image vs. the navigation-heavy category pages. Note: this is a candle warmer, not a memorial candle. May want manual review.
Single product page for a 32-pc gift box — yields one clean product photo vs. the homepage which had nav/banner junk.
Restaurantji listing has 23 review photos of Ely's prepared food and platters — the homepage at elysfinefoods.com was the junk source.
The /menus subpage has plated food photos (kosher entrees, hors d'oeuvres) — the homepage was the original junk URL.
The /meat-cheese category page has gift basket product images; bare homepage returned promotional banner.
Direct sympathy basket product page — most relevant to Neshama use-case and image-rich. Homepage was junk.
English Muffin Sampler Basket product page has the gift basket hero image; homepage returned promo junk.
The /menu page lists dishes with photos — bare homepage was the original junk.
YeahThatsKosher reopening review has multiple in-store photos (pizza, interior); homepage was junk.
YeahThatsKosher tag page aggregates review posts with burger/menu photos — homepage at howiets.ca was junk.
The /catering-platters subpage has cheese-and-charcuterie board photos — exactly what's needed for a shiva-context image. Homepage was junk.
BlogTO Bathurst location review has laffa/shawarma food photos — drlaffa.com homepage returned junk.
Streets of Toronto best-fruit-baskets roundup features Fruitate with arrangement photos; homepage at fruitate.com returned junk.
EventSource roundup of GTA kosher caterers includes Levy's with platter/event photos — homepage at levyskoshercatering.com is a static HTML site with no real photos.
I Keep Kosher listing has pizza/menu photos for this exact location — pizzacafe.ca homepage was junk.

OpenTable listing has restaurant photos (sushi rolls, dining room) — sushiinn.net homepage was junk. Note: this is the Yorkville Sushi Inn, NOT the kosher one — verify with Erin/Jordana that this is the correct vendor.
BlogTO review has falafel/pizza photos — tov-li.com homepage returned junk.
RestaurantGuru Adar page has pastry/prepared-food photos; adar.ca homepage was junk.
MK Kosher's vendor profile has pizza/menu photos — the order-online.ai URL is a third-party ordering shell with no real imagery.

jeromeferrer.ca IS the official Europea site (Chef Jérôme Ferrer is the owner — original task notes were incorrect). The /the-gourmet-table subpage has plated dish photos vs. the bare homepage which was junk.
JoJo Pizza has no standalone website (Uber Eats + IG only). YeahThatsKosher 2025 review has multiple pizza/storefront/menu photos — best available source.
Found correct domain (basketsnstuf.ca, NOT orly-grill which was unrelated). The /gift-baskets subpage has basket product photos.
no usable source — Jordana to research manually. Likely a small home baker; no website, no findable IG, not in any review aggregator.
no usable source — Jordana to research manually. Not findable on standard aggregators or review blogs; likely a very small / informal operation.

Haymishe DOES have a website (haymishebakery.com) — the original task note that they're IG-only is incorrect. Try the homepage; if junk, fall back to https://koshergourmet.ca/haymishe-bakery/ which has product photos.
no usable source — Jordana to research manually. Not findable in any aggregator or directory; likely very small home baker, possibly closed.

The /about-us subpage tends to have more imagery (event photos) than the bare homepage which was the original junk source. Their actual current domain is zbcaterers.com (no www, no .ca/.com mismatch).
